Which operator means 'greater than or equal to'?
Which operator means 'less than or equal to'?
What is the purpose of the 'if' statement?
options: To execute code based on a condition

questions.question: What is a boolean expression?
options: An expression that evaluates to true or false
questions.answer: An expression that evaluates to true or false

What will be the output of: if (!true) { print('A'); } else { print('B'); }

The code inside the 'if' block is executed

options: The case that is always executed
A case that is executed if no other case matches
questions.answer: A case that is executed if no other case matches

questions.question: What happens if a 'break' statement is missing in a 'switch' statement?

options: All of the above depend on the language

questions.question: In a complex conditional expression, what determines the order of operations?
What is the result of (true && (false || true))?
What is the result of (false || (true && false))?
What is short-circuit evaluation?
options: Evaluating only the first part of a conditional
Evaluating only the second part of a conditional
Evaluating only as much of a boolean expression as necessary to determine the result
Evaluating the entire expression regardless of the initial results
questions.answer: Evaluating only as much of a boolean expression as necessary to determine the result

questions.question: What is the advantage of short-circuit evaluation?
options: Both Faster execution and Avoiding errors
questions.answer: Both Faster execution and Avoiding errors

questions.question: Which is more readable: nested if-else or switch?
What is the purpose of using conditionals in programming?
options: To control the flow of execution
questions.answer: To control the flow of execution

questions.question: How do conditionals contribute to decision-making in programs?
options: By executing all possible code paths
By executing specific code blocks based on conditions
questions.answer: By executing specific code blocks based on conditions

questions.question: What is a common use case for conditionals?
Why is it important to write clear and concise conditional statements?
options: To make the code run faster
To make the code harder to understand
To improve code readability and maintainability
To reduce the size of the code
questions.answer: To improve code readability and maintainability

questions.question: What is the benefit of using 'else if' instead of nested 'if' statements?

options: They are required only for single-line statements
They define the scope of the code block
They have no effect on the code
questions.answer: They define the scope of the code block

questions.question: When should you use a 'switch' statement instead of 'if-else if-else'?
options: When there are only two possible conditions
When the conditions involve complex boolean expressions
When comparing a single variable against multiple constant values
questions.answer: When comparing a single variable against multiple constant values

questions.question: How can you combine multiple conditions in an 'if' statement?

options: '=' is for comparison, '==' is for assignment
'=' is for assignment, '==' is for comparison
questions.answer: '=' is for assignment, '==' is for comparison
